short_description: checkout-service OOM-killed across prod-eu-west during Valentine's Day promotion
description: checkout-service pods in prod-eu-west began OOM-killing at 18:42 following the launch of the Valentine's Day 'Valentine's Day Promo' promotional campaign. Session payload size tripled due to bundled product recommendations injected by the pricing-engine. 3 of 5 pods were down simultaneously, causing checkout failures for approximately 11 minutes. Estimated revenue impact: £4,200/min with ~34% cart abandonment spike observed.
resolution_notes: Increased memory limits from 512Mi to 1.5Gi on checkout-service deployment in prod-eu-west. Root cause: promotional recommendation payload injected by pricing-engine added ~800KB per session object. HPA min replicas raised from 3 to 6 for promotional windows. Runbook updated with pre-promotion checklist.

short_description: payment-processor auth token expiry causing silent transaction failures in prod-uk
description: Payment-processor began silently dropping transactions in prod-uk at 09:15 due to an expired OAuth token for the internal payment gateway. Transactions returned HTTP 200 to checkout-service but were not actually processed — customers received order confirmations for orders that did not exist in the payment system. PCI compliance breach risk flagged. Estimated 412 affected transactions over 38 minutes. Revenue impact: approximately £87,000 in unprocessed orders.
resolution_notes: Rotated OAuth token for internal payment gateway. Implemented token expiry alerting with 48h pre-expiry warning via notification-service. All 412 affected orders identified and reprocessed manually. PCI incident report filed. Token rotation added to quarterly compliance runbook.

short_description: inventory-sync batch job failed silently at 02:00 — stock levels stale across 847 stores
description: The 02:00 inventory-sync batch job completed with exit code 0 but wrote zero records to the GKE-side inventory cache due to a connection timeout to the IBM AS/400 warehouse system. No alerting fired because the job technically 'succeeded'. By 06:30, store staff across 847 UK stores were reporting wildly incorrect stock levels — including 14 stores that showed negative stock for premium building materials. Classic case of optimistic error handling.
resolution_notes: Triggered manual inventory-sync re-run at 07:10 after AS/400 connectivity confirmed restored. Root cause: AS/400 maintenance window ran 30 minutes over schedule, coinciding with sync job start. Added record-count validation to batch job — zero-record writes now fail the job explicitly. Alert threshold set at <100 records written. Runbook updated.

short_description: store-pos-system API latency spike affecting all 2,300 stores — Consul mesh misconfiguration
description: Following a routine Consul service mesh config update at 11:00, store-pos-system API response times climbed from 45ms to 4,200ms across all regions. All 2,300 physical stores were affected with POS terminals timing out on stock lookups and basket submissions. In-store queues reported by 14 store managers within 20 minutes. Revenue impact estimated at £6,800/min across the store estate.
resolution_notes: Rolled back Consul config to previous version via GitOps pipeline. Root cause: health check interval reduced from 10s to 1s in the updated config, causing mesh to mark healthy pods as unhealthy under load and reroute traffic. Config change review process updated to require load testing in staging before prod-uk apply.

short_description: product-search returning stale results during scheduled index rebuild — 40-minute degradation window
description: Elasticsearch index rebuild triggered at 14:00 as scheduled, but the index alias swap failed silently, leaving the search service pointing at the old index for the full 40-minute rebuild duration. Customers searching for products were receiving results that did not reflect the day's price updates or new stock arrivals. Approximately 23,000 search queries served stale data.
resolution_notes: Manually triggered alias swap to new index. Root cause: alias swap step failed due to index being in yellow state (one replica unassigned) — swap was coded to abort on non-green index health. Fixed swap logic to allow yellow-state swap with warning. Added post-rebuild alias verification alert.

short_description: order-fulfilment webhook retry storm causing duplicate orders with FulfilPartner-2
description: Order-fulfilment service retry logic triggered a storm of duplicate webhook calls to FulfilPartner-2 after their endpoint returned HTTP 503 for 6 minutes. Retry backoff was configured as linear rather than exponential, resulting in 3,200 duplicate order submissions. This is the third time this has happened. The runbook even has a 'not again' section now.
resolution_notes: Deduplication sweep run against FulfilPartner-2 order queue — 3,187 duplicate orders cancelled. Retry logic updated to exponential backoff with jitter and a 10-retry hard limit. Idempotency key added to all webhook calls. FulfilPartner-2 contacted regarding 503 root cause (their end: DB failover).

short_description: loyalty-service points calculation returning negative balances after bulk redemption event
description: During the 'Ember Grid Rewards February Bonus' event, a race condition in loyalty-service allowed concurrent redemption requests to overdraw point balances, resulting in 892 customer accounts showing negative points. Several customers contacted support after seeing '-4,500 points' on their account page. High visibility customer impact across both UK and EU.
resolution_notes: Deployed hotfix adding database-level row locking on point balance updates. Backfilled 892 affected accounts to zero balance (cannot restore accurate balance due to race condition ambiguity — decision made by CRM team). Customers issued 500 goodwill points each via manual script. Optimistic locking replaced with pessimistic for balance mutations.

short_description: notification-service email queue backed up — customers receiving order confirmation 6 hours late
description: Notification-service email queue depth reached 84,000 messages after a misconfigured rate limit was applied to the SMTP relay connection pool during a config update. Emails were processing at 12/min instead of the expected 1,200/min. Customers were receiving order confirmations hours after placing orders, generating significant support ticket volume.
resolution_notes: Corrected SMTP relay rate limit from 12 to 1,200 connections/min in config. Queue cleared within 2 hours post-fix. Root cause: config value was entered as '12/min' but parsed as per-second, then capped at 12/min by the relay. Added unit validation to notification-service config schema.

short_description: pricing-engine caching stale prices for 3 product categories — customer-visible pricing errors
description: Pricing-engine Redis cache TTL was not invalidated following a bulk price update affecting 1,847 products across the 'Power Tools', 'Home & Outdoor', and 'Outdoor Lighting' categories. Customers were shown incorrect (lower) prices on the e-commerce platform, with the correct price only revealed at checkout. Cart abandonment at checkout increased by 18% during the affected window. Customer complaints to live chat rose sharply.
resolution_notes: Flushed Redis cache for affected product category keys. Bulk price update pipeline now triggers targeted cache invalidation for affected SKU keys. Added cache-aside pattern for pricing reads during bulk update windows. Pricing QA team added post-update smoke test to runbook.

short_description: supplier-integration EDI feed from Supplier-14 timing out — 3,200 SKUs unupdated for 18 hours
description: Supplier-14's EDI endpoint began returning HTTP 504 at 18:00, causing all inbound stock and pricing data for 3,200 SKUs to stall. The integration service's circuit breaker did not open correctly due to a misconfigured threshold, so retry attempts continued hammering the supplier endpoint for 18 hours before alerting fired.
resolution_notes: Supplier-14 restored their EDI endpoint at 11:30. Full resync completed at 12:45. Circuit breaker threshold corrected from 500 errors to 10 errors within 60s. Added supplier-level health dashboard. 18-hour alert gap addressed — supplier timeout alerts now fire after 30 minutes of consecutive failures.

short_description: product-configurator-api returning Dulux codes for Farrow and Ball queries — 4 stores affected
description: product-configurator-api has been returning incorrect brand-mapped colour codes since the 09:15 deployment. Staff in 4 stores reporting customers' custom paint orders are wrong. The service logs show a cache poisoning from a race condition in the colour-map loader. Nobody is entirely sure how the cache loader works. Assigning to whoever last touched it.
resolution_notes: Rolled back product-configurator-api to v2.14.1. Cache poisoning confirmed in colour-map loader — race condition between cache warm-up and first request. Fix scheduled for v2.15.0 with mutex on loader. 4 store managers notified, reprint of 23 order labels arranged.

short_description: GKE node pool autoscaler failing to scale prod-eu-central during morning traffic ramp
description: Prod-eu-central node pool autoscaler did not trigger during the 08:00–09:30 traffic ramp, leaving cluster at minimum node count. Multiple pods entered Pending state due to insufficient CPU. Checkout-service and product-search were most affected, with p99 latency climbing to 8.2 seconds on checkout.
resolution_notes: Manually triggered node pool scale-out to 12 nodes. Root cause: autoscaler was paused by an automated GKE maintenance operation that did not re-enable it. GKE maintenance window config updated to exclude autoscaler pause. Monitoring alert added for autoscaler enabled-state.

short_description: checkout-service SSL certificate expiry causing hard failures in prod-uk
description: The TLS certificate for the checkout-service internal endpoint in prod-uk expired at 03:00, causing all HTTPS connections from the API gateway to fail with a certificate validation error. The certificate was renewed last year and the renewal reminder email went to a distribution list that no longer exists. Discovered at 06:12 when the first store manager called.
resolution_notes: Renewed certificate and redeployed checkout-service. Downtime: 3 hours 2 minutes (03:00–06:02 undetected, 06:02–07:02 active response). Certificate management migrated to cert-manager with automated renewal. All service certificates now tracked in central expiry dashboard with 30-day and 7-day alerts.

short_description: specialty-retail-pos resource starvation — competing with store-pos-system on shared cluster in March
description: Specialty-retail-pos pods began consuming excess CPU following the early March seasonal delivery peak kickoff, starving store-pos-system of resources on the shared prod-uk cluster. Store-pos-system p99 latency rose to 3.1 seconds, impacting in-store POS terminals across 340 specialty-retail-enabled stores.
resolution_notes: Applied CPU limits to specialty-retail-pos pods (500m per pod, max 4 pods). Store-pos-system latency returned to 45ms baseline within 10 minutes of limit application. Resource quotas formalised for both services. Seasonal capacity planning ticket raised for April/May peak.

short_description: inventory-sync desync between GKE and AS/400 — 14:00 batch job partially failed
description: The 14:00 inventory-sync batch job processed only 61% of records before timing out. Remaining 39% of SKUs retained previous batch values. Root cause traced to AS/400 query returning a full table scan instead of a delta query after an index was dropped during a DBA maintenance window.
resolution_notes: AS/400 index rebuilt by DBA team at 15:45. Manual resync job triggered at 16:00, completed at 17:10. Change management process updated to include AS/400 index changes in pre-sync validation checklist.

short_description: notification-service sending duplicate push notifications after order-fulfilment retry
description: Following an order-fulfilment webhook retry event, notification-service received duplicate order completion events and dispatched push notifications twice to ~4,100 customers. Customers received two 'Your order is confirmed!' messages within seconds of each other. Support inbox saw a spike of 'Is my order duplicated?' queries.
resolution_notes: Added idempotency key check in notification-service event consumer — duplicate event IDs are now deduplicated within a 5-minute window. Apology push notification drafted by CRM but ultimately not sent (would have been the third notification). Support FAQ updated.

short_description: product-search cluster split-brain — two Elasticsearch master nodes elected simultaneously
description: A transient network partition in prod-eu-west caused Elasticsearch to elect two master nodes simultaneously, resulting in a split-brain cluster state. Product search returned inconsistent results — approximately 40% of queries hit one shard set, 60% the other. Some products appeared twice, others not at all.
resolution_notes: Forced master election by stopping one master-eligible node, allowing cluster to reform with correct master. Cluster fully green at 17:10. Network partition traced to a misconfigured GKE network policy that was blocking inter-pod Elasticsearch transport traffic intermittently. Network policy corrected. minimum_master_nodes setting reviewed and confirmed correct for 3-node quorum.

short_description: payment-processor latency spike during Saturday peak — p99 reaching 12s
description: Payment-processor p99 latency climbed to 12 seconds during Saturday peak hours (11:00–13:30) in prod-eu-west. Stripe API calls were completing within normal SLA but internal payment gateway calls were queueing. Thread pool exhaustion identified in the internal gateway connector. Cart abandonment at payment step increased by 27% during the window.
resolution_notes: Increased internal gateway connector thread pool from 20 to 80 threads. Immediate latency improvement observed. Root cause: recent Spring Boot upgrade changed default thread pool sizing. Thread pool configuration externalised to ConfigMap for easier tuning. Load test scheduled for following Tuesday to validate.

short_description: supplier-integration portal outage — Supplier-03 and Supplier-07 unreachable for 4 hours
description: Supplier-03 and Supplier-07 both use the same third-party supplier portal, which experienced an outage from 10:00 to 14:00. Combined, these suppliers cover 8,400 SKUs including seasonal outdoor furniture. Inventory data for those SKUs was frozen for the duration. No internal system failure — purely external dependency.
resolution_notes: Supplier portal restored at 14:00. Full resync completed at 14:45. No data loss — last known good values held in cache. Supplier SLA review raised with procurement team. Fallback mechanism to use last-good-value with staleness flag implemented in follow-up ticket.

short_description: checkout-service config rollout applied to prod-eu-west instead of staging — immediate rollback required
description: A configuration change intended for staging was accidentally applied to prod-eu-west after a GitOps pipeline was misconfigured with the wrong target namespace. The change reduced checkout-service connection pool size from 200 to 10, immediately causing connection exhaustion. Checkout failure rate hit 94% within 3 minutes of rollout.
resolution_notes: Rolled back config to previous version via GitOps revert. Service recovered within 2 minutes of rollback. Estimated revenue impact: £4,200/min for 18 minutes. GitOps pipeline now requires explicit namespace confirmation step before prod-eu-west applies. Mandatory PR review added for all production namespace changes.

short_description: loyalty-service pod crashloop — null pointer exception on new tier calculation logic
description: Version 3.8.0 of loyalty-service introduced a new Gold/Platinum tier calculation that did not handle null lifetime_spend values for accounts created before the loyalty programme's 2023 launch. Approximately 340,000 legacy accounts triggered the null pointer exception on first access, causing repeated pod crashes and CrashLoopBackOff state.
resolution_notes: Deployed hotfix v3.8.1 adding null-safe coalescing for lifetime_spend field. Loyalty service stable post-deploy. 340,000 legacy accounts backfilled with lifetime_spend=0. Unit tests added for null-value edge cases. Deployment gate added requiring data migration validation before tier calculation releases.

short_description: GKE cluster upgrade caused CRD incompatibility — multiple services failing to start in staging
description: GKE control plane upgrade to 1.29.4 in staging environment introduced a breaking change in the CRD API version used by the internal service mesh operator. Seven services failed to start post-upgrade. Identified during staging validation, preventing prod rollout. No customer impact.
resolution_notes: Updated service mesh operator to v2.3.1 which supports GKE 1.29.x CRD API versions. All 7 services validated in staging. GKE prod upgrade rescheduled for following week after full regression test pass. GKE upgrade validation checklist expanded to include CRD compatibility check.

short_description: pricing-engine race condition serving wrong promotional price to competing request types
description: Pricing-engine's promotional price resolver had a race condition between standard price requests and bulk promotional calculation jobs. During the 'Spring Refresh' promo launch, approximately 6,200 product pages displayed promotional prices that had not yet gone live. The promotion was supposed to start at noon. It started at 09:47 for those 6,200 SKUs.
resolution_notes: Flushed pricing cache and redeployed pricing-engine with fix for race condition — promotional prices now gated by a time-locked read rather than a write-time flag. Early pricing exposure reviewed by commercial team; decision made to honour prices for orders placed in the window. ~180 orders affected.

short_description: store-pos-system API returning 503 for specialty-retail transactions in prod-uk — till queue buildup
description: Store-pos-system API began returning sporadic 503 errors specifically for specialty-retail transaction types in prod-uk from 13:00 onwards. Root cause traced to a missing index on a recently added specialty-retail product taxonomy column causing full table scans on every transaction type lookup. 218 specialty retail stores affected with till queue times increasing significantly.
resolution_notes: Created index on product_taxonomy_id column concurrently (non-blocking). Query time dropped from 4.2s to 8ms. 503 rate returned to zero within 5 minutes of index creation. Added index validation to deployment pipeline for schema changes.

short_description: order-fulfilment event queue consumer lag exceeding 2 hours — FulfilPartner-1 events processing slowly
description: Order-fulfilment Kafka consumer group for FulfilPartner-1 events fell behind by over 120 minutes after a single malformed message caused the consumer to retry indefinitely without a dead-letter queue configured. All subsequent messages for FulfilPartner-1 were blocked behind the poison message.
resolution_notes: Manually skipped poison message offset. Consumer caught up within 45 minutes. Dead-letter queue implemented for all fulfilment partner consumer groups. Malformed message traced to FulfilPartner-1 sending a UTF-16 encoded payload to a UTF-8 configured topic. Encoding validation added at ingestion.

short_description: product-configurator-api pod unresponsive — liveness probe failing, automatic restart loop observed
description: product-configurator-api entered a liveness probe failure loop in prod-uk. The pod was being restarted every 4 minutes but returning to an unresponsive state each time. Logs show it was loading a 2.1GB colour reference dataset into memory on startup, which exceeded the 2Gi memory limit before the liveness probe timeout. Nobody changed anything. It just started doing this.
resolution_notes: Increased memory limit to 3Gi and startup probe timeout to 120s. Service stable post-restart. Investigation found that the colour reference dataset had grown by 400MB after an automated upstream data update that nobody had documented. Dataset update frequency added to product-configurator-api monitoring. Still nobody fully understands the data loader.

short_description: checkout-service intermittent 500 errors — dependency on loyalty-service timeout misconfigured
description: Checkout-service began returning intermittent 500 errors when loyalty-service was slow to respond. Investigation revealed checkout-service had no timeout configured on its loyalty-service HTTP client — it would wait indefinitely, exhausting its own connection pool. Loyalty-service was healthy; checkout was simply too patient.
resolution_notes: Added 2-second timeout on loyalty-service HTTP client in checkout-service. Implemented circuit breaker pattern for loyalty calls with graceful degradation (skip loyalty points display if unavailable). Connection pool exhaustion no longer possible due to loyalty latency. Timeout configuration audit raised for all service-to-service HTTP clients.

short_description: GKE persistent volume claim stuck in Pending state — storage class quota exceeded in prod-eu-central
description: Three new inventory-sync pods failed to mount persistent volumes in prod-eu-central due to a storage class quota being exceeded. The quota was set at 500GB and had been gradually approached over 6 months. The 14:00 batch job expansion for the spring product catalogue pushed it over. Batch jobs failed to start.
resolution_notes: Storage quota increased to 1TB in prod-eu-central. Cleared 80GB of stale batch job logs from existing volumes. PVCs provisioned and pods started. Storage quota alerting configured at 80% utilisation. Quarterly storage review added to operational calendar.

short_description: product-search relevance scoring degraded — boost config overwritten during index migration
description: Following a planned index migration, product-search relevance boost configuration was not carried forward to the new index, reverting to default Elasticsearch scoring. Premium products were being ranked below generic alternatives. Search quality noticeably degraded — customer-reported and confirmed by internal QA search test suite.
resolution_notes: Re-applied boost configuration to new index. Search QA test suite re-run and passed. Root cause: index migration script did not include boost settings in the index settings export. Migration script updated to include all custom settings. Boost config now version-controlled in Git and validated post-migration.

short_description: security scan flagging high-severity CVE in payment-processor base image — patching required
description: Automated container security scan detected CVE-2026-1247 (CVSS 9.1) in the base image used by payment-processor. Vulnerability exists in the OpenSSL library version bundled in the image. No active exploitation detected, but PCI DSS compliance requires remediation within 72 hours for CVSS >9.0.
resolution_notes: Rebuilt payment-processor image with patched base image (debian:bookworm-slim updated to include OpenSSL 3.0.14). Image scanned clean post-rebuild. Deployed to prod-uk and prod-eu-west with zero-downtime rolling update. Automated base image update pipeline reviewed — update PR was pending approval for 5 days. Approval SLA for security PRs reduced to 4 hours.

short_description: supplier-integration webhook authentication failing for Supplier-22 — HMAC secret rotated without notice
description: Supplier-22 rotated their webhook signing secret without notifying Ember Grid. All inbound webhook payloads from Supplier-22 failed HMAC validation and were rejected from 07:00. 1,200 SKU updates missed across the morning. Stock levels for Supplier-22 hardware range became stale.
resolution_notes: Supplier-22 provided new HMAC secret. Updated in Kubernetes secret store and redeployed supplier-integration. Missed updates resync triggered manually. Supplier onboarding documentation updated to require 48-hour notice for credential rotation. Supplier-22 SLA document updated accordingly.

short_description: order-fulfilment service returning 404 for all requests — ConfigMap deleted during namespace cleanup
description: Order-fulfilment service in prod-eu-central began returning 404 for all API requests at 09:00. Investigation found that a ConfigMap containing fulfilment partner endpoint URLs had been accidentally deleted during a namespace cleanup script run by an engineer who 'thought it was staging'. The service was technically running — it simply had nowhere to send orders.
resolution_notes: ConfigMap restored from Git source of truth. Order-fulfilment service resumed processing. Orders queued during outage (~2,400) processed within 20 minutes post-restore. Namespace cleanup scripts now require two-factor confirmation with namespace name typed explicitly. Separation between staging and prod namespace naming clarified in runbook.

short_description: specialty-retail-pos experiencing high latency — April spring rush causing CPU throttling
description: Specialty-retail-pos pods hit their CPU limits across prod-uk and prod-eu-west during the April spring rush peak (10:00–14:00 on Saturday). CPU throttling caused transaction processing times to rise from 200ms to 3.8 seconds. 340 specialty retail stores reported slow tills. Revenue impact estimated at £2,100/min across affected stores.
resolution_notes: Increased specialty-retail-pos CPU limits from 500m to 2000m and scaled to 8 replicas. Latency dropped to 180ms within 5 minutes. Seasonal HPA scaling profile created for April–May period: min 6 replicas, max 12, CPU target 60%. Capacity planning for 2027 spring season added to Q1 planning cycle.

short_description: checkout-service Kafka producer unable to connect to broker — certificates expired in prod-eu-central
description: Checkout-service in prod-eu-central lost connectivity to the Kafka broker at 06:00 due to expired mTLS certificates. Orders placed via the EU central platform were accepted at the frontend but silently dropped after checkout submission — no order was created. Customers received success confirmation screens for orders that did not exist.
resolution_notes: Rotated mTLS certificates for prod-eu-central Kafka clients. Service connectivity restored. Affected time window: 06:00–08:44. Order creation audit run — 1,847 customer orders silently dropped. All customers contacted directly with apology and orders manually recreated. Kafka client certificates added to cert-manager inventory.

short_description: pricing-engine stale cache causing £0.00 prices on 47 SKUs — displayed on homepage
description: A pricing-engine cache warm-up failure left 47 newly launched SKUs with a price of £0.00 in the Redis cache. The homepage featured two of these products in a 'New Arrivals' banner. The £0.00 price was displayed on the product listing pages and visible to all users for approximately 22 minutes. Social media screenshots followed promptly.
resolution_notes: Flushed cache for affected SKUs and re-warmed from pricing database. Correct prices restored within 5 minutes of identification. 12 orders placed at £0.00 identified — commercial decision made to honour prices as goodwill gesture. Cache warm-up now validates price > 0 before writing. New SKU launch pipeline requires pricing validation before cache population.

short_description: inventory-sync job consuming 100% CPU on AS/400 interface node — impacting warehouse operations
description: The 02:00 inventory-sync batch job introduced a new full-catalogue reconciliation query that caused the AS/400 interface node to pin at 100% CPU for 3 hours, impacting warehouse management system responsiveness during the morning shift. Warehouse staff reporting slow terminal response across the distribution centre. Not a GKE issue — the AS/400 side was simply overwhelmed.
resolution_notes: Terminated the runaway reconciliation query. Reverted to delta-only sync for the 02:00 job. Full reconciliation query re-written with pagination — processes 5,000 records per chunk with 500ms delay between chunks. Full reconciliation now scheduled for 01:00 Sunday only. AS/400 team informed and co-reviewed new query plan.

short_description: loyalty-service reward redemption API returning 500 — DB connection pool exhausted
description: Loyalty-service reward redemption endpoint began returning 500 errors across prod-eu-west and prod-uk simultaneously at 14:30 during a promoted double-points redemption event. Database connection pool of 20 connections was exhausted within seconds of the event launch. Approximately 45,000 customers unable to redeem rewards during the peak 30-minute window.
resolution_notes: Increased DB connection pool from 20 to 100 connections on loyalty-service. Added read replica routing for non-mutating loyalty queries. Connection pool sizing added to promotional event pre-flight checklist. Future high-traffic redemption events require load test in staging against projected concurrent user count.

short_description: store-pos-system health check endpoint returning 200 with degraded internals — misleading monitoring
description: Store-pos-system health check endpoint was returning HTTP 200 while the internal database connection was in a degraded state. Monitoring showed all-green while POS terminals in 412 stores experienced intermittent failures. The health check was apparently checking whether the HTTP server was alive, not whether it was capable of doing anything useful.
resolution_notes: Fixed health check endpoint to include database connectivity and dependency checks. Restarted pods after fixing degraded DB connection (stale connection pool entries). 412 stores restored to full functionality. Comprehensive health check standards documented and applied to all services via shared health check library.

short_description: product-search CPU spike during nightly index rebuild — disrupting co-located services
description: Nightly Elasticsearch index rebuild job consumed all available CPU on its GKE node at 02:00, causing co-located pods (notification-service and loyalty-service) to be CPU-throttled during the 40-minute rebuild window. Scheduled notification sends were delayed and loyalty tier calculations backed up.
resolution_notes: Added node affinity rules to ensure Elasticsearch pods schedule on dedicated node pool during index rebuilds. Rebuild job CPU limit set to 4000m to prevent node saturation. Notification and loyalty services confirmed unaffected in post-fix overnight window.

short_description: payment-processor Stripe webhook delivery failures — missing IP allowlist update after network change
description: Stripe webhook deliveries to payment-processor began failing at 15:00 following a GKE egress IP change caused by a node pool recreation. The new egress IPs were not added to the Stripe webhook IP allowlist. Payment status updates (captured, refunded, disputed) were not being received, causing order status to remain in 'processing' state indefinitely.
resolution_notes: Updated Stripe webhook IP allowlist with new GKE egress IPs. Webhook delivery restored. Stripe replayed failed webhooks covering the outage window — all order statuses updated correctly. Node pool recreation runbook updated to include payment gateway IP allowlist update as a mandatory step.

short_description: product-configurator-api unavailable in prod-eu-west — deployment failed due to missing image tag
description: A deployment of product-configurator-api v2.15.0 to prod-eu-west failed because the Docker image tag referenced in the Helm chart did not exist in the container registry. The CI pipeline had succeeded but the push step silently failed due to a registry authentication issue. Prod-eu-west fell back to no running pods as the rolling update terminated old pods before the issue was caught.
resolution_notes: Rolled back Helm release to v2.14.1 immediately. Registry auth issue traced to an expired service account key for the CI pipeline's registry push role. Key rotated and image successfully pushed. Deployment pipeline updated to verify image existence in registry before initiating rolling update.

short_description: supplier-integration EDI batch failure — Supplier-31 schema version mismatch
description: Supplier-31 upgraded their EDI message format from EDIFACT D96A to D01B without coordinated migration. Supplier-integration began rejecting all inbound messages from Supplier-31, causing a backlog of 890 purchase order confirmations and 1,450 ASN (advance ship notice) records. Warehouse unable to pre-receive Supplier-31 deliveries.
resolution_notes: Deployed EDIFACT D01B parser support for Supplier-31 channel. Processed backlog of 2,340 queued messages. Supplier onboarding agreement updated to require 30-day notice for EDI schema version changes. Schema version detection added to integration to auto-select correct parser.

short_description: GKE secret sync failing — external-secrets operator out of date in staging
description: External-secrets operator in staging failed to sync secrets from GCP Secret Manager following a GCP API deprecation. Staging pods started failing to mount secrets, blocking all deployment testing. No production impact, but pipeline for 6 teams was blocked.
resolution_notes: Upgraded external-secrets operator from v0.9.4 to v0.10.2 in staging. All secret syncs restored. GCP deprecated API endpoint was the v1beta1 Secret Manager API. Production was already on v0.10.1 and unaffected. Operator version parity policy between staging and prod tightened to max 1 minor version lag.

short_description: checkout-service failing for EU customers with non-ASCII characters in delivery address
description: Checkout-service began rejecting order submissions from EU customers where the delivery address contained non-ASCII characters (e.g., German umlauts, French accents). Error: 'invalid address field — ASCII encoding required'. Root cause: a recent sanitisation middleware was too aggressive, stripping and then rejecting non-ASCII characters.
resolution_notes: Removed overly aggressive ASCII sanitisation middleware. Address fields now accept full UTF-8. Deployed hotfix. Affected EU customers identified and checkout retry emails sent. Test coverage expanded to include non-ASCII address scenarios for all checkout acceptance tests.

short_description: notification-service SMS sends failing — Twilio account suspended due to unpaid invoice
description: Notification-service SMS sends began failing at 00:00 with Twilio returning HTTP 402. Investigation revealed the Twilio account invoice had not been paid due to an expired credit card on the billing account. SMS notifications for order dispatches, delivery updates, and loyalty alerts were all silently dropped for 11 hours before the on-call engineer noticed elevated error rates.
resolution_notes: Updated Twilio billing card. Account reinstated within 10 minutes of payment. SMS backlog of 34,000 messages processed within 90 minutes. Twilio 402 error code added to alerting rules with P1 escalation. Billing card expiry alerts set up for all third-party service accounts.

short_description: specialty-retail-pos service account permissions revoked — pods unable to read GCP secrets
description: Specialty-retail-pos pods began failing startup in prod-eu-west after a GCP IAM policy cleanup revoked read permissions from the service account used by the external-secrets operator for that namespace. Pods entered CrashLoopBackOff as they could not retrieve DB credentials. 89 EU specialty retail stores affected.
resolution_notes: Restored secretmanager.secretAccessor role to specialty-retail-pos service account. Pods restarted and secrets mounted correctly. IAM cleanup process updated to include dry-run validation against running workload service accounts before removing permissions.

short_description: order-fulfilment duplicate orders to FulfilPartner-3 — retry logic triggered by 200ms latency spike
description: Order-fulfilment service sent duplicate orders to FulfilPartner-3 after a brief 200ms latency spike caused the HTTP client to retry a request that had already been successfully received by the partner. FulfilPartner-3's API was not idempotent. 780 duplicate orders dispatched.
resolution_notes: Worked with FulfilPartner-3 to cancel 780 duplicate orders. FulfilPartner-3 has agreed to implement idempotency key support — ETA 4 weeks. In the interim, order-fulfilment now maintains a local sent-order cache for 60 seconds to prevent immediate re-sends. Retry timeout increased from 200ms to 5s to avoid false positives.

short_description: product-search autocomplete returning empty results for all queries — Redis cache flush
description: Product-search autocomplete feature returned zero results for all search queries from 14:00 after a Redis cache flush was run on the wrong Redis instance — the autocomplete suggestion cache rather than the planned pricing cache. Customers saw empty autocomplete dropdowns across the e-commerce platform.
resolution_notes: Re-warmed autocomplete Redis cache from product index. Cache populated within 45 minutes. Root cause: Redis instance naming convention was ambiguous — both instances had 'cache' in the name. Renamed Redis instances to explicitly reflect their function. Flush command runbook now requires naming the target instance explicitly.

short_description: inventory-sync overwriting live stock levels with test data — staging sync job pointed at prod DB
description: A developer testing a new inventory-sync configuration in staging accidentally pointed the sync job at the prod-uk inventory database. The job ran successfully — overwriting live stock levels for 12,000 SKUs with test fixture values. Several products showed stock of 99,999. A few showed stock of -1. One showed stock of 'banana', which is impressive.
resolution_notes: Restored prod-uk inventory from the 02:00 backup and applied delta sync from AS/400 to catch up to current values. 12,000 SKU stock levels validated against AS/400 source of truth. Network policy added to prevent staging pods from reaching prod databases. Staging config now uses a dedicated staging DB endpoint — hard-coded and not overridable via env vars.

short_description: pricing-engine pod evicted due to node memory pressure — promotional pricing unavailable for 25 minutes
description: Pricing-engine pod in prod-uk was evicted due to node memory pressure at 12:00 during a 'Bank Holiday Weekend' promotional campaign. The pod had no PodDisruptionBudget and no HPA minimum replica count set, meaning there was a period of 25 minutes with zero running pricing-engine pods while scheduling resolved. E-commerce customers saw an error at the checkout price summary step.
resolution_notes: Pod rescheduled on new node. PodDisruptionBudget created for pricing-engine with minAvailable: 2. HPA minimum replicas set to 3 for pricing-engine. Node memory over-commitment policy reviewed — node pool using burstable instance types replaced with standard instances for critical services.

short_description: checkout-service cart session data loss — Redis eviction policy misconfigured to allkeys-lru
description: Customer cart sessions were being silently evicted from Redis during the Bank Holiday traffic peak after the Redis eviction policy was inadvertently set to allkeys-lru during a performance tuning session. Customers with active carts lost their session data and were shown an empty cart. Estimated 18,000 affected sessions over 2 hours. Cart abandonment spiked to 41%.
resolution_notes: Changed Redis eviction policy to volatile-lru (only evict keys with TTL set) and increased Redis memory allocation. Cart session keys do not have TTL so are now never evicted by policy. 18,000 sessions lost — no recovery possible. Customer comms handled by CRM with £5 voucher offer. Eviction policy added to Redis change management checklist.

short_description: store-pos-system TLS handshake failures after GKE certificate rotation — 298 EU central stores offline
description: Automated GKE certificate rotation in prod-eu-central generated new internal TLS certificates, but the store-pos-system trust store was not updated with the new CA certificate. All 298 EU central stores lost API connectivity simultaneously at 04:00, with POS terminals offline for the morning shift start. Revenue impact estimated at £3,100/min across affected stores from 07:00 when stores opened.
resolution_notes: Updated store-pos-system trust store with new CA certificate and performed rolling restart. All 298 stores reconnected within 12 minutes of deployment. GKE certificate rotation runbook updated to include trust store update step for all services using internal mTLS. Pre-rotation checklist now requires trust store compatibility validation.

short_description: loyalty-service tier upgrade emails sending to wrong customer segment — query filter inverted
description: A bulk email campaign triggering 'Congratulations, you've reached Gold tier!' messages was sent to customers who had just dropped below Gold tier rather than those who had achieved it. A boolean filter in the tier-change query was inverted in a recent refactor. Approximately 14,200 customers received a celebratory demotion notification.
resolution_notes: Deployed fix with corrected boolean filter. Correct Gold tier upgrade emails sent to the actual 9,800 qualifying customers. Apology email drafted for the 14,200 incorrectly contacted customers — 500 bonus points offered as goodwill. Query logic for segmented campaigns now requires peer review and QA sign-off before execution.

short_description: supplier-integration connection pool exhausted — 47 concurrent supplier feeds during end-of-month sync
description: Supplier-integration service exhausted its database connection pool during the end-of-month bulk sync window when all 47 supplier feeds triggered simultaneously. The pool limit of 30 connections was insufficient for concurrent processing. 23 supplier feeds failed mid-sync, leaving partial data for those suppliers in the inventory system.
resolution_notes: Increased DB connection pool to 80. Implemented supplier feed staggering — feeds now start with 2-minute offsets rather than simultaneously. Re-ran 23 failed syncs manually. No inventory data corruption — partial writes were rolled back. End-of-month sync schedule formalised with staggering built in.

short_description: product-configurator-api RGB output values exceeding 255 for light colour calculations — incorrect mixing ratios
description: Product-configurator-api began returning RGB values above 255 for very light colours (near-white RAL codes) following a floating-point rounding change in v2.15.1. Mixing ratio outputs were therefore incorrect for those shades. 6 stores reported customer complaints about product configuration results. The bug was in a function that, to nobody's great surprise, was not covered by any unit tests.
resolution_notes: Deployed v2.15.2 with RGB value clamping to 0–255 range and corrected rounding logic. 6 affected stores notified; customers offered free remixes. Unit tests added for boundary values in colour calculation functions. Test coverage for product-configurator-api is now, charitably, slightly better than before.

short_description: order-fulfilment webhook TLS certificate presenting wrong SAN — FulfilPartner-1 rejecting connections
description: Order-fulfilment service began receiving TLS certificate validation errors from FulfilPartner-1 following a certificate renewal that generated a cert with the wrong Subject Alternative Name. The new certificate had the staging domain in its SAN rather than the production domain. FulfilPartner-1 orders halted for 1 hour 45 minutes.
resolution_notes: Reissued TLS certificate with correct production SAN. Deployed to order-fulfilment service. FulfilPartner-1 connectivity restored. Queued orders processed within 30 minutes. Certificate generation pipeline updated to include SAN validation step comparing generated cert against expected domain list.

short_description: GKE pod disruption during node upgrade — checkout-service briefly below minimum replica count
description: During a scheduled GKE node upgrade in prod-eu-west, the PodDisruptionBudget for checkout-service was not respected due to a Kubernetes version compatibility issue. Two pods were drained simultaneously, briefly leaving checkout-service with 1 of 6 replicas running. Error rate spiked to 62% for 4 minutes.
resolution_notes: Pods rescheduled automatically within 4 minutes. PDB compatibility issue traced to a known bug in GKE 1.29.3 where maxUnavailable is misread during node drain. Upgraded node pool to 1.29.5 which includes the fix. GKE upgrade windows for checkout-service moved to 02:00–03:00 Sunday to minimise revenue exposure.

short_description: pricing-engine EU VAT rate calculation returning UK rates for EU customers post-deployment
description: Pricing-engine v4.2.0 deployment introduced a regression where the VAT rate resolver defaulted to UK VAT (20%) for all customers regardless of locale, applying incorrect rates to EU customers. EU customers in checkout were seeing higher prices than expected. Visible at checkout for approximately 3 hours before identification.
resolution_notes: Rolled back pricing-engine to v4.1.9. VAT locale resolver regression confirmed — locale context was not being passed to the new rate resolver function. Fix implemented and tested in staging. Redeployed v4.2.1 with fix at 14:00. Affected EU orders audited — 340 orders overcharged; refund credits issued automatically.

short_description: specialty-retail-pos May Bank Holiday traffic causing OOM kills — spring peak underestimated
description: Specialty-retail-pos pods across prod-uk began OOM-killing at 10:30 on the May Bank Holiday Monday — peak spring specialty retail store shopping day. Despite the April capacity planning work, the actual traffic exceeded projections by 40%. 340 specialty retail stores experienced intermittent POS failures during the busiest trading period of the year.
resolution_notes: Increased memory limits to 4Gi and scaled to 14 replicas. OOM kills stopped within 8 minutes of change. Root cause: seasonal traffic model did not account for Bank Holiday multiplier effect combined with warm weather. Traffic was 40% above sunny-weekend baseline. 2026 spring model updated with Bank Holiday weather-adjusted factor. Next Bank Holiday pre-scaling runbook created.

short_description: inventory-sync failing for 14 stores following store system firmware update — protocol mismatch
description: 14 stores received a firmware update to their local inventory management terminals that changed the API protocol version from v2 to v3. Inventory-sync was not updated to support v3, causing sync failures for those stores. Stock levels for those locations became stale within 24 hours.
resolution_notes: Deployed inventory-sync update with v3 protocol support and backward compatibility for v2. All 14 stores re-synced successfully. Firmware rollout process updated to require inventory-sync compatibility check before firmware deployment. Multi-version protocol support now standard for inventory-sync.

short_description: notification-service push token database growing unbounded — disk pressure on prod-eu-west
description: The push notification token table has been growing without any TTL or cleanup policy since the notification-service launched in 2023. The table now contains 47 million rows including millions of invalidated tokens. Disk usage on the notification-service database hit 92%, causing slow query alerts and intermittent write failures.
resolution_notes: Ran batch deletion of tokens not seen in >180 days — removed 31 million rows, freeing 68GB. Disk usage dropped to 34%. Implemented automated weekly cleanup job for tokens older than 90 days. Data retention policy formalised for notification-service and added to operational data governance register.

short_description: product-search index rebuild blocking writes — caused by misconfigured refresh interval
description: During the nightly index rebuild, Elasticsearch's refresh_interval was set to -1 (disabled) to improve rebuild performance, but a code bug failed to reset it to the default 1s value post-rebuild. Product catalogue updates made by the merchandising team between 03:00 and 09:00 were not reflected in search results.
resolution_notes: Manually reset refresh_interval to 1s. Index refreshed and all pending writes became visible. Code bug in rebuild script fixed — refresh interval reset is now in a finally block so it executes even on error. Post-rebuild validation added to check refresh_interval value.

short_description: payment-processor failing 3DS authentication for EU customers — new SCA regulation enforcement
description: EU payment processor began strictly enforcing updated Strong Customer Authentication requirements from May 6th. Payment-processor was not updated to send the required additional authentication fields, causing 3DS challenges to fail for all EU card payments. EU e-commerce revenue was effectively zero from 00:00 until the fix was deployed.
resolution_notes: Deployed payment-processor v5.3.1 with updated 3DS authentication fields required by new SCA enforcement. EU card payments restored. Regulatory compliance calendar reviewed — SCA enforcement date was known but implementation ticket was not prioritised. Post-incident compliance review scheduled. Estimated revenue impact: £4,200/min for 225 minutes.

short_description: checkout-service session token validation failing for mobile app users — JWT library version mismatch
description: Mobile app users began receiving 'Invalid session' errors at checkout following a checkout-service deployment that upgraded the JWT validation library. The new library version changed default algorithm preferences, rejecting tokens signed with RS256 by the mobile app's auth flow. Web users unaffected as they use a different token path.
resolution_notes: Deployed fix explicitly configuring RS256 as accepted algorithm in the new JWT library. Mobile checkout restored. Root cause: library upgrade followed a 'secure defaults' change that disabled RS256 in favour of ES256 without announcement in the changelog. Mobile app auth migration to ES256 added to Q3 roadmap.

short_description: supplier-integration feed processing paused — Kafka broker leader election during rolling upgrade
description: A rolling upgrade of the Kafka cluster caused a leader election event that lasted 3 minutes and 40 seconds, during which supplier-integration consumers lost their broker connections. Consumers did not reconnect automatically — a bug in the consumer reconnect logic caused them to exit rather than retry. 6 supplier feeds missed during the window.
resolution_notes: Restarted supplier-integration consumers. Missed messages replayed from Kafka offsets — no data loss. Consumer reconnect bug fixed — exponential backoff retry loop now handles broker unavailability. Kafka rolling upgrade procedure updated to monitor consumer group lag during upgrade and alert if lag exceeds 60 seconds.

short_description: loyalty-service failing to award points for click-and-collect orders — missing order type mapping
description: Click-and-collect orders introduced a new order type code ('CC') that was not mapped in the loyalty-service points calculation logic. All click-and-collect orders since the feature launched 3 weeks prior awarded zero points. Estimated 62,000 affected orders. The issue was raised by a customer on social media who noticed their points balance hadn't moved despite regular orders.
resolution_notes: Deployed fix adding CC order type to loyalty points calculation mapping. Backfill job run to award missed points to 62,000 affected accounts — completed in 4 hours. Customer comms sent explaining the issue and confirming points added. New order type launch process updated to require loyalty-service mapping as a prerequisite.

short_description: store-pos-system intermittent 502 errors — ingress controller memory leak under high request volume
description: Store-pos-system ingress controller in prod-uk began exhibiting a memory leak under sustained high request volumes during the May Bank Holiday week, gradually degrading response reliability. 502 error rate climbed from 0.01% to 3.8% over 48 hours. 890 stores affected with intermittent till errors before the leak was identified.
resolution_notes: Rolled ingress controller pods to clear memory. Upgraded ingress-nginx to v1.11.2 which patches the identified memory leak (upstream issue #10847). Post-upgrade memory utilisation stable at 340Mi vs pre-fix 1.8Gi. Memory alerting on ingress controller pods set at 1Gi threshold.

short_description: order-fulfilment service config map missing fulfilment SLA thresholds — defaulting to zero
description: Following a namespace migration in prod-eu-central, the order-fulfilment SLA threshold ConfigMap was not recreated. The service defaulted all SLA values to zero, causing every order to be immediately flagged as SLA-breached. The SLA breach alerting system proceeded to fire 14,000 alerts in 90 minutes, which did not make anyone's evening better.
resolution_notes: Recreated SLA threshold ConfigMap from Git. Service restarted. False SLA breach alerts cleared. Alert suppression applied during migration windows added to namespace migration runbook. Namespace migration checklist now includes ConfigMap inventory validation step.

short_description: product-search missing 4,200 SKUs after catalogue import bug — products not appearing in search
description: A product catalogue import script introduced in v3.7.0 had an off-by-one error in its batch pagination logic, consistently skipping the last record of each 100-record batch. Over the 4,200-SKU spring catalog range import, every hundredth product was silently omitted from the Elasticsearch index. These products were searchable by direct URL but invisible in search results.
resolution_notes: Fixed off-by-one error in pagination logic and re-ran catalogue import. All 4,200 SKUs now indexed correctly. Import validation step added to check imported record count against source record count post-import. Discrepancy >0.1% now fails the import job with an alert.

short_description: product-configurator-api latency spike — upstream RAL code database responding slowly
description: product-configurator-api response times climbed to 8.4 seconds average in prod-uk from 14:00, traced to the upstream RAL colour code reference database being under maintenance by the third-party provider. No internal failures — the service was simply waiting politely for a very slow response from a vendor who had not announced a maintenance window. Classic.
resolution_notes: Upstream RAL database restored at 16:45. Product-configurator-api latency returned to 120ms baseline. Timeout added to upstream RAL database HTTP client — 3 seconds, with fallback to cached RAL values. Vendor contract reviewed to include maintenance notification requirement.

short_description: checkout-service prod-eu-central pod count drifted from desired state — HPA misconfiguration
description: Checkout-service in prod-eu-central was running 2 pods against a desired minimum of 6 for three days before anyone noticed. HPA had been misconfigured with a maxReplicas lower than minReplicas following a copy-paste error in a Helm values file. The cluster enforced maxReplicas (2) as the ceiling, overriding minReplicas. Service remained functional under normal load but had no headroom.
resolution_notes: Corrected HPA values — minReplicas: 6, maxReplicas: 20. Service scaled to 6 pods within 2 minutes. Validation added to Helm chart to assert maxReplicas >= minReplicas at render time. Automated replica count drift alert added for all production services.

short_description: inventory-sync alerting on false desync — timezone offset bug in comparison logic
description: Inventory-sync began firing desync alerts for all stores in prod-eu-central at 02:00 on May 10th, despite stock levels being accurate. Investigation found a daylight saving time transition had introduced a 1-hour offset in the sync comparison timestamps, making all records appear 1 hour stale beyond the allowed threshold. 2,300 alerts fired. All false positives.
resolution_notes: Fixed timestamp comparison to use UTC throughout — DST offset was being applied to comparison timestamps in the EU central zone. Alert storm suppressed after confirmation all records were valid. All datetime handling in inventory-sync audited and converted to UTC. DST transition added to quarterly operational watch calendar.

short_description: notification-service push provider rate limit hit — bulk promotional campaign not throttled
description: A bulk push notification campaign for the May Bank Holiday sale dispatched 1.4 million notifications in a single burst, hitting the push notification provider's rate limit of 500,000/hour. Notifications were queued at the provider level and delivered over 3 hours rather than the intended instant delivery. Time-sensitive flash sale messaging lost its time-sensitivity.

short_description: pricing-engine failing health checks in prod-eu-central — Redis connection refused after network policy change
description: Pricing-engine in prod-eu-central began failing liveness probes after a network policy update blocked egress to the Redis instance on port 6379. The network policy change was intended to tighten security but inadvertently included the pricing-engine Redis CIDR in the blocked range. Pods entered CrashLoopBackOff.
resolution_notes: Updated network policy to allow egress from pricing-engine to Redis on port 6379. Pods restarted and stabilised. Network policy change review process updated to include egress impact analysis for all dependent services before applying.

short_description: specialty-retail-pos product advisory data returning 404 — content CDN cache purge wiped static assets
description: Specialty-retail-pos in-store advisory panels (used by staff to show customers product care information) began returning 404 for all advisory images and PDFs following a CDN cache purge that removed static assets without revalidation. 340 specialty retail stores affected. Technically a minor issue — customers can still buy the products, just without being told how to use them incorrectly.
resolution_notes: Re-uploaded static assets to CDN origin. Cache warmed via pre-fetching script. CDN purge runbook updated to include origin-availability validation before purge and automatic re-warm post-purge. Static asset deployment pipeline now includes CDN availability verification step.

short_description: store-pos-system deployment rollout stalled at 40% — readiness probe timeout too aggressive
description: A rolling deployment of store-pos-system v8.4.0 in prod-uk stalled at 40% rollout because the readiness probe timeout was set to 5 seconds, while the new version's startup initialisation takes 12 seconds. New pods were consistently marked not-ready and the rollout paused. 60% of stores continued on the old version, 40% experienced intermittent readiness cycling.
resolution_notes: Updated readiness probe initial delay to 15 seconds and timeout to 10 seconds. Deployment resumed and completed successfully. Startup probe added to all services with initialisation times >5 seconds to separate startup latency from runtime readiness. Probe configuration review added to deployment checklist.

short_description: supplier-integration rate limiting Supplier-09 API calls — token bucket not reset after restart
description: Supplier-09 API calls were being aggressively rate-limited by supplier-integration after a pod restart reset the in-memory token bucket to zero rather than restoring its correct state. All API calls queued for Supplier-09 were throttled to 1 request per 60 seconds instead of the configured 100/minute. 4,200 stock updates delayed by over 6 hours.
resolution_notes: Restarted pod with corrected token bucket initialisation — bucket now starts full on pod start rather than empty. Rate limiting state externalised to Redis to survive pod restarts. Supplier-09 backlog cleared within 40 minutes of fix. Supplier API rate limit configuration audited across all 47 suppliers.

short_description: checkout-service unable to reach payment-processor — internal DNS resolution failure in prod-eu-west
description: Checkout-service in prod-eu-west lost DNS resolution for payment-processor.prod-eu-west.svc.cluster.local from 21:30. CoreDNS pod had been evicted due to node memory pressure and did not reschedule for 18 minutes due to node pool capacity. All checkout attempts failed at the payment step. Revenue impact: £4,200/min for 18 minutes.
resolution_notes: CoreDNS pod rescheduled after node pool scaled out. DNS resolution restored. CoreDNS pods now have PriorityClass set to system-cluster-critical to prevent eviction under memory pressure. Node memory alert threshold lowered to 75% to provide earlier warning before eviction threshold is reached.

short_description: loyalty-service read replica lag causing points balance inconsistency — 90-second replication delay
description: Loyalty-service began routing all read queries to a read replica that had developed a 90-second replication lag from the primary. Customers checking their points balance immediately after a transaction were seeing their pre-transaction balance. Not data loss — just a confusing user experience that generated significant support contact.
resolution_notes: Replication lag traced to a long-running analytics query on the replica that was blocking the applier thread. Killed query. Lag cleared within 5 minutes. Analytics queries moved to a dedicated analytics replica. Replication lag alert set at 30 seconds for loyalty-service read replicas.

short_description: order-fulfilment retry queue building up — FulfilPartner-2 degraded performance undetected
description: FulfilPartner-2's API began responding slowly (8–15 second response times) without returning error codes. Order-fulfilment retry queue grew from 0 to 34,000 items over 6 hours as retries accumulated for slow-but-not-failing requests. Circuit breaker did not open because all requests eventually succeeded — just very slowly.

short_description: product-search EU central returning results only in German — locale header ignored after config push
description: Product-search in prod-eu-central began returning all results with German-language product descriptions regardless of the Accept-Language header sent by the client. A configuration push had hardcoded the locale to 'de-DE' in the search query builder. Customers in the Netherlands, Belgium, and France were receiving German product descriptions.

short_description: product-configurator-api deployment blocked — DAST scan finding critical injection vulnerability in v2.16.0
description: Automated DAST security scan on product-configurator-api v2.16.0 in staging detected a potential server-side injection vulnerability in the RAL code input parser. Deployment to production blocked pending security review. Not currently exploitable in production as v2.15.2 is running, but the finding must be resolved before promotion.

short_description: pricing-engine prod-uk showing weekend promotional prices on Monday morning — schedule rollover bug
description: Pricing-engine in prod-uk failed to roll off the weekend Bank Holiday promotional pricing at 00:00 on Monday. Customers shopping Monday morning continued to see Bank Holiday sale prices for 3 hours. The promotional schedule rollover job had silently failed due to a race condition in the price-schedule evaluator introduced in v4.2.1.

short_description: specialty-retail-pos unable to process loyalty redemptions at till — dependency timeout on loyalty-service
description: Specialty-retail-pos in-store POS loyalty redemption flow began timing out from 09:00 on May 10th. Loyalty-service was healthy and responding within SLA, but specialty-retail-pos had a 500ms timeout configured for loyalty calls — half the loyalty-service p99 response time under current spring peak load. Customers unable to use Ember Grid Rewards points in-store at specialty retail stores.

short_description: notification-service unsubscribe links broken in email templates after base URL change
description: Following a domain configuration change, notification-service email templates were not updated with the new base URL. All email unsubscribe links in order confirmations, loyalty emails, and promotional messages are pointing to a 404 page. Compliance risk: broken unsubscribe links violate GDPR email requirements. Volume: approximately 120,000 emails sent since the URL change 4 days ago.

short_description: inventory-sync job running 3x longer than baseline — AS/400 statistics not updated post-spring-load
description: The inventory-sync batch job has been running 180 minutes instead of the expected 60 minutes for the past 5 days. AS/400 DBA team confirmed that table statistics have not been updated since the spring catalogue expansion, causing the query optimiser to choose inefficient query plans. No data errors — just very slow.

short_description: store-pos-system prod-eu-central returning stale product descriptions — translation cache TTL set to 0
description: Store-pos-system in prod-eu-central is serving product descriptions from a translation cache where the TTL was inadvertently set to 0 seconds, disabling caching entirely. The service is now making a live translation API call per request, causing p99 latency to climb to 2.1 seconds and translation API costs to escalate by approximately 800% versus the weekly baseline.

short_description: checkout-service prod-uk memory usage trending upward — suspected memory leak in session handler
description: Checkout-service pods in prod-uk have been steadily increasing memory usage by approximately 50Mi per hour over the past 48 hours without a corresponding traffic increase. Current usage at 1.7Gi against a 2Gi limit. Pods will begin OOM-killing within an estimated 6 hours if the trend continues. Being monitored; hotfix investigation underway.

short_description: payment-processor audit log writes failing — CloudSQL disk at 95% capacity
description: Payment-processor PCI audit log writes to CloudSQL began failing at 22:00 on May 10th due to the database disk reaching 95% capacity. The disk has been growing steadily since the audit log retention policy was changed from 90 days to 365 days in February without a corresponding disk resize. PCI compliance requires audit logs to be written successfully — current state is a compliance risk.

short_description: supplier-integration Supplier-41 sending malformed JSON — integration service throwing unhandled exception
description: Supplier-41 began sending malformed JSON payloads (trailing commas) from their updated product catalogue API at 18:00. Supplier-integration throws an unhandled exception on parse failure and the pod restarts, clearing the backlog of other supplier feeds in memory. 12 other supplier feeds are being disrupted each time the Supplier-41 message is retried.

short_description: loyalty-service audit trail missing for 3,200 point redemption transactions — logging misconfiguration
description: Loyalty-service audit logging for point redemption events was silently disabled for 72 hours following a logging library configuration change that set the audit log level to OFF for the redemption event handler class. 3,200 transactions are unaudited. GDPR and internal compliance requirements mandate full audit trails for loyalty financial transactions.

short_description: store-pos-system barcode scanner integration returning duplicate scan events — debounce removed in v8.4.1
description: Store-pos-system v8.4.1 removed a 200ms debounce on barcode scanner input events as part of a 'performance improvement'. Barcode scanners in 340 stores began registering every item twice, doubling quantities in customer baskets. Most customers noticed. Some did not, and were overcharged. The performance improvement was not worth it.

short_description: product-search faceted filters returning incorrect product counts — aggregation query bug in v3.8.0
description: Product-search faceted filter counts (e.g. 'In Stock: 142', 'On Sale: 37') are returning inflated counts following the v3.8.0 deployment. The aggregation query was changed to use a global aggregation scope instead of a filtered scope, causing counts to reflect the entire catalogue rather than the current search context. Customer-visible cosmetic issue affecting filter usability.

short_description: order-fulfilment SLA dashboard showing all orders as breached — metrics clock skew between nodes
description: The order-fulfilment SLA monitoring dashboard began showing 100% SLA breach rate at 08:00 on May 10th. Investigation confirmed actual fulfilment SLAs are being met — a clock skew of 4 minutes and 12 seconds between two GKE nodes hosting the metrics aggregator is causing order timestamps to appear older than they are. No operational impact, but the dashboard is causing unnecessary escalations.

short_description: specialty-retail-pos till receipt printer integration timing out on large mixed baskets
description: Specialty-retail-pos receipt printer integration is timing out when generating receipts for baskets containing more than 40 line items — common during the spring bulk-buy season when customers purchase large quantities of bulk merchandise. The receipt generation service has a 3-second timeout that is consistently exceeded for large baskets. Tills complete the transaction but print no receipt, requiring staff to manually reprint.

short_description: notification-service email open tracking pixels returning 404 — path prefix misconfigured after CDN update
description: Email open-rate tracking pixels embedded in notification-service outbound emails are returning 404 following a CDN path prefix change. Marketing analytics for all email campaigns sent in the past 6 days are showing 0% open rates, causing significant concern in the marketing team. No customer-facing impact — emails are delivered correctly, only the tracking is broken.
resolution_notes: Updated CDN path prefix for tracking pixel route from /track/ to /t/v2/ to match new CDN configuration. Pixel endpoint verified returning 200. Historical open-rate data for the 6-day gap is unrecoverable — marketing team informed. CDN path changes now require notification-service tracking config review as a mandatory step.

short_description: pricing-engine promotional flag not clearing for 12 SKUs after campaign end — residual discount applied
description: Twelve SKUs continued serving a 25% promotional discount for 9 hours after the associated campaign ended, due to a promotional flag not being cleared in the pricing-engine cache on campaign expiry. The affected SKUs were in the 'Premium Home & Outdoor' category. Approximately 84 orders placed at incorrect discounted prices during the window.
resolution_notes: Manually cleared promotional flags for 12 affected SKUs. Correct pricing restored. Campaign expiry logic updated to explicitly flush promotional flags from cache on expiry rather than relying on TTL. 84 affected orders reviewed — commercial team decision to honour discount prices as margin impact was acceptable.

short_description: inventory-sync delta query returning zero rows for new stores — store_id range gap in query filter
description: Three new stores (IDs 2301–2303) opened in late April were not included in the inventory-sync delta query due to a hardcoded store_id upper bound of 2300 in the sync job configuration. Stock levels for all three new stores have never been synced to GKE. Staff in those stores have been working with manually entered stock data for 2 weeks without realising the sync was absent.

short_description: supplier-integration failing to parse Supplier-18 product images — MIME type validation rejecting WebP
description: Supplier-18 migrated their product image delivery from JPEG to WebP format without prior notice. Supplier-integration's inbound image validator only accepts JPEG and PNG MIME types, causing all Supplier-18 product images to be rejected since May 7th. Approximately 2,800 product listings in the catalogue are now showing placeholder images instead of product photos.

short_description: loyalty-service Ember Grid Rewards app widget showing points in euros for UK customers — locale fallback bug
description: The Ember Grid Rewards points balance widget in the mobile app is displaying points with a '€' currency symbol for UK-registered customers following a locale resolver change in loyalty-service v3.9.0. Points themselves are correct — only the symbol is wrong. UK customers are understandably confused about why their loyalty points appear to have converted to euros. No financial impact.

short_description: checkout-service OOM kill - pod restarted 2 times in 12 minutes (INC0043000)
description: checkout-service reported OOM kill in prod-uk; pod restarted 2 times in 12 minutes. Root cause spans two services: upstream pricing-engine degraded first and forced retries through checkout-service. Kubernetes reported OOMKilled after the container hit its memory limit during a traffic burst. On-call observed 40 affected requests and about 3 percent degradation in checkout conversion before mitigation.

short_description: payment-processor CPU throttling - CFS throttle ratio reached 19 percent (INC0043001)
description: payment-processor reported CPU throttling in staging-uk; CFS throttle ratio reached 19 percent. Root cause spans two services: upstream loyalty-service degraded first and forced retries through payment-processor. Container CPU usage hit the cgroup limit and request latency climbed while pods stayed Ready. On-call observed 57 affected requests and about 8 percent degradation in authorisation flow before mitigation.

short_description: product-search disk pressure - PVC usage reached 90 percent (INC0043002)
description: product-search reported disk pressure in prod-eu-west; PVC usage reached 90 percent. Root cause spans two services: upstream inventory-sync degraded first and forced retries through product-search. Log growth and retained batch output filled the persistent volume faster than rotation could reclaim space. On-call observed 74 affected requests and about 13 percent degradation in search results before mitigation.

short_description: order-fulfilment node eviction - 5 pods evicted from one node pool (INC0043003)

short_description: pricing-engine node eviction - 10 pods evicted from one node pool (INC0043459)
